questions.scenario: You're at 90 Oath Gauge. A tankbuster is coming in 5 seconds.
questions.question: What should you do with your Oath?
questions.explanation: At 90 Oath with a tankbuster coming, use Sheltron now. It provides mitigation for the buster and prevents overcapping at 100.

questions.scenario: You need to mitigate damage for a party member who's about to take a targeted hit.
questions.question: Which ability should you use?
questions.explanation: Intervention spends Oath to give Sheltron's mitigation to another party member. Cover takes damage for them, which is riskier.

questions.scenario: You're off-tanking and haven't been hit in a while. Your Oath is at 20.
questions.question: Why isn't your Oath building?
questions.explanation: Oath Gauge builds from auto-attacks, not combos. If you're not in melee range auto-attacking, you won't generate Oath.

questions.scenario: Holy Sheltron just expired. Tank autos are hitting hard.
questions.question: What does Holy Sheltron provide besides the block?
questions.explanation: Holy Sheltron provides a heal-over-time (Knight's Resolve) and mitigation, making it excellent for sustained damage.

questions.scenario: Combat just started. Your Oath is at 0.
questions.question: What's the fastest way to build Oath?
questions.explanation: Oath only builds from auto-attacks in combat. Stay in melee range and let auto-attacks generate gauge.

questions.scenario: Big tankbuster coming. Rampart is on CD. Sentinel is available.
questions.question: Should you use Sentinel?
questions.explanation: Sentinel provides 30% mitigation - use it for big tankbusters. Don't save it for emergencies that might not come.

questions.scenario: You have Rampart, Sentinel, and Sheltron available. Huge tankbuster incoming.
questions.question: What's the best mitigation approach?
questions.explanation: Sentinel (30%) + Sheltron is usually enough. Save Rampart for the next buster. Stacking all three is overkill and wastes CDs.

questions.scenario: Large trash pull in a dungeon. Many enemies auto-attacking you.
questions.question: Which cooldown synergizes best with Sheltron here?
questions.explanation: Bulwark increases block rate, which synergizes with Sheltron's guaranteed blocks for sustained auto-attack mitigation.

questions.scenario: You used Sentinel at the start of the fight. It's now coming off cooldown.
questions.question: When should you use it again?
questions.explanation: Plan your Sentinel usage around major damage events like tankbusters. Using it proactively is better than reactively.

questions.scenario: Rampart has 5 seconds remaining. Another small hit incoming.
questions.question: Should you add another cooldown?
questions.explanation: For small hits, one mitigation is enough. Don't stack cooldowns on minor damage - save them for bigger hits.

questions.scenario: Boss is casting a massive tankbuster. You're at 30% HP.
questions.question: Should you use Hallowed Ground?
questions.explanation: At 30% with a massive hit coming, Hallowed Ground guarantees survival. It's the best tank invuln - take 0 damage.

questions.scenario: Hallowed Ground is on a 420 second (7 minute) cooldown.
questions.question: How should this affect your usage?
questions.explanation: In longer fights, using Hallowed early means you might get a second use. Plan around the fight timeline.

questions.scenario: Huge wall-to-wall pull in a dungeon. Healer is struggling.
questions.question: Is Hallowed Ground useful here?
questions.explanation: Hallowed Ground is excellent for big dungeon pulls - 10 seconds of immunity gives healer time to stabilize.

questions.scenario: Comparing PLD's invuln to other tanks.
questions.question: What makes Hallowed Ground special?
questions.explanation: Hallowed Ground makes you fully invulnerable - no HP drop like Superbolide, no healing requirement like Living Dead.

questions.scenario: You used Hallowed Ground. 10 seconds of invulnerability.
questions.question: What should you focus on during this window?
questions.explanation: During Hallowed, you can't take damage. Use this window for maximum offense - no need for defensive actions.

questions.scenario: Fight just started. When should you use Fight or Flight?
questions.question: What's the optimal timing?
questions.explanation: Use Fight or Flight after your first GCD to maximize the 20s window. Using before the GCD wastes time.

questions.scenario: Goring Blade combo finisher available. Fight or Flight is active.
questions.question: What does Goring Blade apply?
questions.explanation: Goring Blade applies a powerful DoT. Using it during Fight or Flight means the DoT does 25% more damage.

questions.scenario: You just used Royal Authority. 3 Atonement stacks available.
questions.question: What's the correct Atonement chain order?
questions.explanation: The chain is Atonement > Supplication > Sepulchre. Each hit in the chain does increasing damage.

questions.scenario: Fight or Flight is up. You have Expiacion and Circle of Scorn available.
questions.question: How should you use these oGCDs?
questions.explanation: Use oGCDs during Fight or Flight to benefit from the 25% damage boost. Pack your burst into the window.

questions.scenario: Fight or Flight has 3 seconds remaining. You need to move.
questions.question: What should you prioritize?
questions.explanation: Holy Spirit is instant during Requiescat, but even without, it maintains DPS while moving during the FoF window.

questions.scenario: Requiescat just came off cooldown. You're mid-physical combo.
questions.question: When should you use Requiescat?
questions.explanation: Finish your physical combo first, then use Requiescat to transition to magic phase. Don't interrupt combos.

questions.scenario: Requiescat is active. You need to cast Holy Spirit.
questions.question: What's the cast time during Requiescat?
questions.explanation: Requiescat makes Holy Spirit and Holy Circle instant cast. This is why magic phase is great for movement.

questions.scenario: You have 4 Requiescat stacks. What combo should you start?
questions.question: What's the correct sequence?
questions.explanation: The Confiteor combo (Confiteor > Faith > Truth > Valor) is your strongest magic burst. Use it to consume stacks.

questions.scenario: You're in the middle of Blade of Faith. Boss becomes untargetable.
questions.question: What happens to your combo?
questions.explanation: The Blade combo is a true combo chain - it continues even through brief untargetable phases. Don't panic.

questions.scenario: Lots of movement required for upcoming mechanic.
questions.question: Which phase handles movement better?
questions.explanation: Magic phase with Requiescat gives instant casts. Save magic phase for high-movement mechanics when possible.

questions.scenario: Divine Veil is active on you. Party-wide damage incoming.
questions.question: What triggers Divine Veil's party shield?
questions.explanation: Divine Veil activates when you receive healing. Coordinate with healers to ensure the shield deploys before damage.

questions.scenario: A party member is about to take a lethal hit. Cover is available.
questions.question: What does Cover do?
questions.explanation: Cover makes you take all physical damage for the target. Useful but dangerous - you need to survive the hit.

questions.scenario: Party stacking behind you for a raidwide. Passage of Arms available.
questions.question: How does Passage of Arms work?
questions.explanation: Passage of Arms creates a cone behind you that reduces damage. It's channeled but the buff persists briefly after canceling.

questions.scenario: Healer is dead. Tank is at 30% HP.
questions.question: When is Clemency appropriate?
questions.explanation: Clemency is a DPS loss (GCD cost + MP). Use it only in emergencies when healers can't help.

questions.scenario: You used Divine Veil but it expired without activating.
questions.question: What went wrong?
questions.explanation: Divine Veil requires you to receive healing to activate. If no heal comes, the ability is wasted.

questions.scenario: Tank swap mechanic. You're currently off-tank.
questions.question: What's the correct swap sequence?
questions.explanation: Proper tank swap: you Provoke, then co-tank Shirks to you. This ensures clean aggro transfer without enmity ping-pong.

questions.scenario: Expiacion is off cooldown. Fight or Flight is coming in 10 seconds.
questions.question: Should you use Expiacion now?
questions.explanation: If FoF is close and you won't lose a use, wait. If holding would cost a use over the fight, use it now.

questions.scenario: Multiple enemies. Circle of Scorn available.
questions.question: What makes Circle of Scorn valuable in AoE?
questions.explanation: Circle of Scorn applies a DoT to all enemies hit. In AoE situations, this is significant damage over time.

questions.scenario: Need to close gap to boss. You have 2 Intervene charges.
questions.question: How should you manage Intervene charges?
questions.explanation: Keep at least one charge for mobility. Use excess charges during burst windows for damage.

questions.scenario: You're main tank. Debuff requires a swap. Co-tank Provokes.
questions.question: What should you do after?
questions.explanation: After being Provoked off, Shirk to the new main tank. This gives them a comfortable aggro lead.
